reviews.review_title: Didn't click at first, now a favorite
reviews.review_description: Really excellent art direction all around, especially later areas. I found the variety in different class loadouts, upgrades and items great. When I first tried playing I bounced off after some slight frustrations with difficulty or how easily I died, on returning to it a year later it clicked for me better and I've since played through it dozens of times.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic experience but some big bugs..
reviews.review_description: I love the diversity of the classes and the harsh gameplay but about 1 out of every 3 runs is ruined by an enemy that can't be killed that stays invincible even after you die, quit the game, etc. Often this occurs right near the exit so when you try to leave you just get shot to death. Also I just had two runs in a row where the last soul purifier did not appear. I lost around 150K souls each time which is about 5 levels or 75% of your earned advancements. Perhaps the devs can add another soul purifier before the final boss fight so that the last 2/3rds of game progression isn't lost.

purchased a second time just so that i could have this game on the go :) what a great game.

i would love a way to sync my game from one platform to another.

people complaining about difficulty... i had no issue playing a fresh game 1st run beat no problem (no upgrades, not even a pistol).
this is somewhat of a peaker shooter, but really you can rush them down, especially when you're on a shotgun or similar close range weapon.
people complaining about aiming quality, they are just wrong and bad at aiming with iron sights, though obviously a holo sight or laser makes it super simple.

difficulty scaling can be oppressive & the final stage of difficulty selection doesn't feel fun anymore since the main scaling option is health. and just ripping clip after clip 10x to kill an elite is not a fun game.

* I purchased and play this game via PCVR first
(an objectively better experience due to lower loading time, much better draw distance, and way less stutter)

It’s obviously not perfect, but it's the best shooter rougelike in VR still today.

Great job & looking forward to more from the developers

I love the aesthetics of this game, the praying is a fun way to interact with anything.
However the gameplay loop is surprisingly bland. The enemies are aimbots, you wait your turn in the cover, that's basically it. You have limited amount of ammo, you can't really hit anything without a scope.
There is no spice in the random generated maps, they essentially feel the same.

I've had this game for a couple years and its super fun, the boss is an insane twist, but really fun once you get the hang of fighting him. Would love to see multi-player added! Get like 3 or 4 man groups and up the spawn rate of enemies maybe? My friends and ai would love to be able to go through runs together!
Love this game!

reviews.review_title: Quit the game out of frustration..
reviews.review_description: I’m not very good in playing games, but after I tried my hardest looking for a flashlight or anything for those sewer levels, I decided I don’t want a headache. Aside from that, probably the one of the best, with lots of playability.

reviews.review_title: Game that LOOK nice but is trash, again
reviews.review_description: literaly all ennemies kill in less than 2 hit, WHILE ALL SHOOTING FROM LONG RANGE, while you are slow, with a smooth barrel gun that cant even 1 hit on heads other than on the 2 first tutorial ennemies, and while you got a ammo pouch that keep moving around and wont let you grab a full magazine if you didnt empty the previous one completly, definition of a fake "hard game" that is not hard but just broken and unbalanced

Having played for a while, unlocking all the classes and what not, it was fun.
Until they decide to push one of their numerous updates, which always has a chance to delete your entire save file
They try to make the game challenging by making damage high, and cover essential but this all falls short when you can't even shoot over cover because of their atrocious hitboxes!
What couldve been a good game, went down the drain with non existent devs

reviews.review_title: One I Keep Coming Back To
reviews.review_description:
It’s been a while since I played The Light Brigade, but I really enjoyed this game.

Everything about it just clicked for me. The atmosphere, the gameplay, and the overall feel. It’s one of those experiences that sticks with you even after you step away for a bit.

Easy 5 stars from me. I’m definitely planning to jump back in soon.
This game is fabulous ! I really enjoyed it ! The learning curve is not easy and high grinding, but I had plenty of fun ! Try it !!!

reviews.review_title: My favorite rouge like game on vr!!
reviews.review_description: I got the game thinking it would be nothin special but this game is surprisingly fun and the combat feels nice and the guns are just wow. By far my favorite class is the one with the time watch and revolver. I be getting so many headshots. Not that far yet tho😅 trying to max the revolver guy out tho.💪 9.6/10 game
Nicely made roguelike but lacks multiplayer. Opened it to quickly check out, closed after two hours
I tried this game years ago, and didn’t get it. Spent some time in it and now I do! My new fave!!!
Don't get me wrong its a relly good game but it just doesn't get upadets anymore.

The game play in its self isn't bad but you move so slowly it takes forever to get anywhere. And yes I know you can teleport, but its so buggy it ends up getting you killed 9/10 times. The bow is also buggy as it will get stuck on just about anything in a twenty mile radius.
Its simple. But the game play loop is great. I hope the devs add some content, more maps would be awesome. And a co-op mode would be a game changer

I’ve played this game for a few hours and I’m already sold. The class progression feels smooth and encourages you to try other classes. The only issue I’ve found is with the Hunter class. The recurve bow you’re given is for a right handed shooter, it would need to be mirrored to feel comfortable shooting left handed. There are many options for control changes but it would be great if there was a setting specifically for the bow to flip it for a left handed shooter. Otherwise, it’s a fun game that I’m excited to keep playing.
I don’t wanna play for like four hours straight let me pause😫

this is a very good game from the gun play all the way to the art. But like always in these games jts run off the mill difculty and then a massive f you wall at the end with a stupid hard boss with a very complex move pattern and practialy undogeable attacks. I was prepered for this type of boss but it still sucks fighting it. Do i recommend it? yea kinda but dont waste your time compleationg the game unless your a commpleatinalis.
On Quest 3, turn on high resolution mode instead of quality mode in the settings. Looks better and you get a frame boost too.

reviews.review_title: Don‘t think, just buy. No brainer
reviews.review_description: This is one of the very best vr games. The mixed vr mode is a cherry on top of a game that you can replay forever.
It’s a good rouge like for the money
Love these  Roguelike games purchased it ages back and keep going back to it never ceases to be entertaining.❤️❤️❤️

My biggest grip with VR is there are "vr games" then "games in vr". One actually uses the technology as part of the game where as the other  just uses it as a fancier computer monitor. This is the former rather than the latter. I'm going to be completely honest here the story is kind of trite and uninteresting but the gameplay is excellent. There are 4 "worlds" each randomly generated. It's a rougelike, kill the mobs and pick up power ups. Weapons have realistic loading, racking, and unloading. Moving and shooting through the levels feels good. This is my first FPS on a VR device and  I will start when I have a full battery and play till it's dead.

(game received free from horizon+)
This is probably one of the best VR roguelikes, pick your class, shoot enemies, explore the area, collect tarot cards and attachments, repeat, finish your run or die trying, collect upgrade points for your class, and do it all over again, wide variety of classes allows for several playstyles, shotgunners, snipers, rifles, and my favorite, the saboteur, however, it's not perfect, sometimes worldgen has some mistakes, walls not acting as such, sometimes enemies are completely unshootable, and the final boss WILL curbstomp you until you learn how it works

Absolute joy to play. They nailed the guns, the enemies and the style. It’s slick, different each time and has enough surprises and progression ti keep you coming back. I had it on PCVR but bought the Quest version as well. Its identical. Superb
I absolutely love this game I actually can’t tell you how many times I’ve played it over and over again. I just wish that there were more weapons, more enemies, more abilities, new scenery, etc.. ￼

the game is exactly fun i've played it for a very long time already and it's lovely but adding multiplayer might enhance the experience it would definitely make things harder though because with the more players in the lobby the health and damage the enemy's get

Original review below, but after a lot of time away, it seems much more polished now. Some of them still pop up and there are blemishes, but this a good game with good fun factor. OG REVIEW below but upgrading the score to 4 stars 

Origin 2 Star review 

Devs, please take a deep look to clean this game up. 

First of all, when it's working well, it's awesome. 
Unfortunately, It is a difficult game made unnecessarily MORE difficult through cheap deaths and glitchy mechanics. 

Pros: 
-Multiple classes and upgrades to pursue your stronger playstyles
-BEAUTIFUL art style yielding great graphics as long as you dig the style (I do) 
- Mixed reality mode is very fun (but see cons as well)
-Guns are fun and varied, and all have different reloads which is fun too. 


Cons
-Normal mode requires you to deposite XP, which at first was interesting, but makes the stakes too high for  game with as many issues as this. It also creates a balance issue which has me playing the mixed reality mode more because it's easier to level up do to different XP leveling mechanic. In the main game if you die before depositing the souls (xp) you lose them all. This is frankly BS. Just make us lose 40% or 50% if you want to incite a challenge. Whenever a death feels unwarranted i feel my playtime was WASTED.
-Mixed reality mode needs some serious help. In the MIDDLE of a match my boundry CHNAGED (not the meta one, the game has you set a boundry of play even within that). This led from what started as a GREAT run to immediate death. Very cheap, immersion breaking, game breaking
-Also game breaking in mixed reality, they occasionally can shoot me through the walls, but I can't. 
-Balance issues in main game mode: the fog makes it hard to see enemies at a certain distance. It's no fun when we are the same distance and they pick me off as if there is no fog for me, only them. 
-GAME BREAKING: In each mode, I have had issues with reaching for side arm, and somehow pulling up ammo instead. Death. The grips on each gun have given me issues registering if I am trying to hold it, or reload it. Nothing like an intense gunfight leading to your death because the gun you tried to reload twirls around in your hands because you try to grab the bolt.

Updating my initial review from 2 to 5 stars!

I found it boring in the beginning to always go through the same stages but there are actually a lot of variations and upgrades, new enemies, unexpected developments. After some runs I love it now, perfect follow up after too much of In Death. I longed for a similar game but different and more evolved and that's exactly what Light Brigade delivers! It's quite challenging too.
I would say it’s worth the money

the graphics , gun mechanics , sounds, etc make it the best game on the  quest store. However being a new gamer it is extremely difficult , lose everything you get, have to start over every time .  The best game on here is terrible

reviews.review_title: The game I was looking for
reviews.review_description:
First review I’ve ever written for a Quest game but this game deserves it. After trying a few other titles like Into the Radius and In Death: Unchained (which are both great games in their own right) this game combined all the elements into a game that I consistently enjoy and find myself coming back to. 

I was never a fan of roguelike games, but it works really well for this medium, and the leveling is still very satisfying. Each session you play feels different depending on which tarot cards you activate as you progress, and looting in general is quite satisfying. Great aesthetic as well. My only complaint could be that the graphics might not be optimized for Quest 3, but that has never been a bother. Can’t wait to see what this studio makes next.

reviews.review_title: Underrated Gem If You Take Your Time
reviews.review_description:
I've had this game for over two years now, and I still find myself coming back to it regularly. It scratches a very specific itch that no other game has managed to hit for me.

I honestly don’t understand all the negative comments — maybe some players are just trying to rush through it. This game rewards patience and strategy. Once you learn how to play it properly, it becomes incredibly engaging and fun.

Take your time. Fight enemies one at a time, tackle each level slowly, and you'll start to appreciate the depth. I especially love the risk-reward system — when you die, you lose everything, but the fact that you can still recover it adds real tension and satisfaction.

Highly recommend if you enjoy methodical, immersive gameplay.

reviews.review_title: A Full Game With VR medium utilised!
reviews.review_description: Just buy and play it if you r interested in a roguelike shooter!
It took a bit to pick up momentum for me, but once I unlocked a few classes it really took off. Very fun game. Excellent balance of difficulty.

reviews.review_title: I have some ideas for new classes
reviews.review_description: I LOVE THE HELL OUT OF THIS GAME but after a bit it's kind of boring with the same weapons, even the Hunter and saboteur got boring after I leveled them up to level 12, but I had a couple ideas the first one is the magician, since there's magic in the game and different types of effects what if there was a character with a magic wand (different from the ones almost every class has) that can do different things with a press of a button aka the B/Y button, it has different magic and is very AOE based and status effect based... That would be super fun, the next class idea is shadow walker similar to magician in terms of "magic" but now you can throw balls of shadows and such, a move called shadow creep allows you to appear behind an unaware enemy with teleport which can be used with shadow blade (a small secondary kind of like the throwing knives but it can be used a normal weapon up close), the shadow blade deals good damage and does extra damage when you hit someone while they're unaware or behind them (yes it can be thrown)... I HOPE YOU LIKE THE IDEASSSSSSS, KEEP UP THE AMAZING WORK
Extremely fun game with a great story

So start with positive: good art style and sound with music... Design of weapons and npc are nice and gameplay is solid and some zones are incredibly looking and when you get lucky with cards and drops...you feel like one man army
Now the negative: there is no proper ending just three flavour of texts same bosses and same zones... And feels empty in storytelling (yea i know theres some flashing story bits after you die) but no proper ending nor seeing any changes in camp when you rescue soldiers... Basically the game stuck in loop.... so... after while it gets boring

So overall? It's fine.... Get it when it heavily discounted
I would LOVE this game a lot more if multiplayer was added

reviews.review_title: Don’t EVER make another vr game again!
reviews.review_description:
EVERY SINGLE TIME I TRY TO GIVE THIS GAME A CHANCE, I JUST CONTINUE TO FIND MORE REASONS TO DESPISE IT AS MUCH AS I DO!!!

Let me get the positives out of the way. 

Minus the fact that we have the thousandth WW2 simulator, as if we don’t have enough of THOSE already… I enjoy the narrative and the atmosphere of this also being a fantasy game.  I enjoy the praying mechanic, even though it doesn’t always do what it’s supposed to do… And I enjoy the souls mechanic where you just collect souls as they fly towards your hand.  

Here’s where the positives end.  

What I don’t like about the souls mechanic is that you need to survive until the end of NOT ONE, but TWO levels in order to purify them.  For context, this game is way more difficult than it seems.  You need to take it slow, you need to explore as much as you can so you can find more cash and more souls.  It’s really easy to gain souls, but assuring that they won’t be lost is a completely different story.  With how persistent the enemy AI is, you will be caught off guard SUPER EASILY.  With any roguelite, it is expected that you’re gonna die over and over again but you will have better stats.  What’s NOT expected is you could be taking your time, killing the enemies and making massive gains on the battlefield, and all of a sudden you get a game over.  Because you need to make it to the end of the second area and purify your souls, you can potentially gain all of those souls and forget to purify them, or even get super close to the end but still die, AND ALL OF YOUR EFFORTS WILL BE FOR NOTHING WHEN YOU GET A GAME OVER! This has happened WAY more often than I have ever wanted and it makes me feel like I just wasted so much time collecting souls, because they could just be lost just like that.

Even the most challenging roguelites like Until You Fall, Compound and HellSweeper have more fair outside-of-the-run currency mechanics than this.  When you die, your ghost stands right at the spot where you died.  In order to gain the souls back after a death, you need to make it back to that spot, NO MATTER HOW FAR YOU WERE INTO THE LEVEL AT THAT POINT, so you can pray and gain the souls back.  Speaking of trying to make it back to a certain point of the level after dying, Why. On. Earth. Are the enemies able to respawn after you have previously killed them just because YOU got killed?  I don’t care that this is a roguelite, I KILLED THOSE ENEMIES ALREADY, WHY AM I HAVING TO DEAL WITH THEM AGAIN?? This is another prime example of the game making me feel like my efforts have been for nothing.  It also makes me feel like I could have just been speed running the level rather than being tactical, WHICH IS WHAT THE GAME EXPECTS ME TO BE DOING ANYWAY!

On that note of trying to make it back to the point where you died previously in a level, apparently dying and making it back to your ghost and praying to get your souls back is a way to gain more souls so you can purify more of them, especially if you’re playing on a harder difficulty.  This is one of the dumbest game mechanics I have ever seen.  Last time I checked, isn’t the best way to gain currency or XP in a roguelite, to try to WIN a run?  To make progress? Let alone to beat the final boss?  NOT DYING?  The only time I could understand this being useful was in Galaga.  GALAGA!!! AS IN THE 1981 ORIGINAL!!!

The life system is also poorly explained. When you lose a life, you go into a soul’s light mode where the entire level turns grey, all of the enemy way points are given to you and you are on the risk of getting a game over. The game does absolutely nothing to tell you that you’re at a risk of getting a game over unless you turn your hand over and see that you are at a WEAK status. I can’t even see how many lives I have left. The way I was able to figure this out literally was by experiment. Because I actually want to win most of the time so all of this is just poorly explained to me even when my lives are maxed out.

The recoil.  OH MY GOD THE RECOIL IS SO BAD!!!  I swear, you vr devs need to stop flexing about how good your vr physics are.  It’s 2025, Everybody can make Gorilla tag nowadays.  The recoil is so strong, WHILE BEING DUAL HANDLED BY THE WAY that it feels like when you’re firing a semi-automatic, you might as well be firing a shototototo gun from that one meme video on YouTube.  I would love to feel like I am shooting a semi-automatic and not feel like my body is so weak that I can’t even hold the gun still while I’m holding shoot, meanwhile the enemies don’t get to face that problem like me. 

One of the things that drew me into this game was that the enemies were apparently much smarter than the average enemy npc… That is when the enemies are actually trying to kill you.  Why can’t the enemies just stay still when they spot me?  What I mean by that is why can’t they just keep trying to shoot at me and not try to run to a different position EVEN THOUGH I AM STANDING RIGHT IN THEIR FACE?

Oh and why do the enemies get to punch me and I can’t punch them back? How in the world is this fair?

Let's talk about the fact that these enemies could heal. Now I'm not super mad about the fact that the enemies could heal and this might be because I was playing on the hardest difficulty, but I do find it a tad unfair that you can shoot the enemies by 10-20 damage but they just heal 40 HP at a time. Unless if you have lightening fast reflexes, you cannot reload your weapon fast enough to actually kill these guys.

One could look at that part of the review and just say, "Well just go into your settings and turn on Disable Manual Bolt Handling?" Which is a solid argument... WHICH IS WHAT I WOULD SAY IF IT ACTUALLY DID WHAT IT'S SUPPOSED TO DO! When you turn on this setting, it doesn't allow you to just slam a cartridge into the gun and start firing. Nononono. What ACTUALLY happens is you try to reload the gun by inserting the cartridge in, AND THE BOLT IS LOCKED SO YOU CAN'T RELOAD ANYMORE!

Also why is the setting called 'Disable Manual Bolt Handling'? Why couldn't you just name it 'Manual Bolt Handling' or 'Allow Manual Bolt Handling?’ Why do I have to do reverse psychology in other to make the settings the way I want them? WHY ARE THEY EVEN CALLED DISABLE SO AND SO? YOU TURN THEM ON AND YOU WOULD THINK THAT THEY WOULD ENABLE SOMETHING!!!

Anyway, the combat prayer doesn’t do what it’s supposed to do half of the time, meaning I could have the prayer maxed out but it won’t let me see where the enemies are most of the time.  Even though I am standing right behind the entrance for the level I would do a prayer and the enemies would appear to not be detected even though I walk through the entrance and all of a sudden an enemy is standing really close anyway, VERY USELESS!

I wasn’t even gonna add more complaints to my review for this game until I discovered that SOMETIMES the game will just glitch out and one of the enemies I’m trying to kill won’t even take damage at all! The bullets will just faze right through him BUT HE CAN STILL DAMAGE ME?!?! (This alone makes me hate the game as much as I do. This game’s been out for 2 years and the developers couldn’t do anything about THIS bug? Are you kidding me?)

OTHER TIMES the game will glitch out and I can’t even dual hold my weapons and aim properly. 

The loading screens are terribly long, the game for whatever reason feels that it’s necessary to load up a story image even though I have seen it at least 5 times already, by this point my immersion has been completely broken and this game might as well be like the Sonic 06 of VR games when it comes to the loading screens. I LITERALLY HAVE FORGOTTEN TO RELOAD MY WEAPONS IN THE LAST TEMPLE 3 TIMES IN A ROW ONE TIME BECAUSE THE LOADING SCREENS ARE SO LONG THAT I LOSE TRACK OF WHAT I NEED TO DO HALF THE TIME!

Oh and sometimes the loading screens will just fail and the game will soft lock. At this point I’m legitimately questioning if these issues are gonna be fixed, or if the developers even care at all to fix them. 2 years.

The draw distance… WHY ON EARTH DO THE ENEMIES SHOW UP VISUALLY BUT SOME OF THE STRUCTURES HAVE TO POP IN WHEN YOU GET CLOSER? WHY DOES THIS GAME SHARE A SIMILAR PROBLEM THAT HITMAN 3 VR RELOADED HAS???

The Last Temple, aka the hub world is confusing to navigate through, I could not for the life of me understand why the developers could not just have the rest of the settings in a flat window for people to scroll through.  On that note, Is it really necessary to have to find a statue and pray to it, JUST TO ADJUST THE DIFFICULTY SETTINGS?  

There’s a mode known as Sun Run which is supposed to be a mode of the game where if you are not able to handle the regular runs, a sun run is a nice alternative. The sun run in a nutshell are an easier version of a random set of levels based on one of the environments on repeat. They do get harder but it’s a much easier way to farm souls and skill points, and the levels are much more linear. 

My biggest issue with the sun run is how it needs to be accessed. You can’t just access it like you would the normal run, the shooting range or the Mixed Reality mode where you would pray and the mode would activate. What you need to do (WHAT LITERALLY EVERYONE PLAYING THIS GAME NEEDS TO DO) is contribute to unlock the sun run by depositing your souls. There’s a structure that tells you the percentage of how many souls people have deposited to unlock the sun run… I’m not sure why the sun run isn’t just available right from the start, but that’s not my actual complaint. My actual complaint is once the sun run is unlocked, (which can take a really long time) it’s only available for a few days. So let me get this straight… if I don’t want to do the traditional run, I gotta either SUFFER through dying multiple times in the normal run (because keep in mind some people aren’t able to handle the normal run that well) JUST TO ACCESS THE EASIER MODE FOR ONLY 2-3 DAYS?!?!?!  Also there’s a point where the sun run expires and what happens is every time you look at the sun structure that gives the percentage  the soul percentage goes down really slowly, BUT THE SUN RUN IS NOT AVAILABLE. SO IF MY POWER GOES OUT ONE DAY AND THE SUB RUN IS AVAILABLE, I COULD LOSE MY POWER FOR A WEEK OR SO AND NOT ENJOY THE SUN RUN! 

This is also the only game in vr I have ever played where it will not save if you have a low battery. So if you’re charging your headset with a power bank attached because you got a low battery notification, don’t even think of your progress actually making a difference. 

Why is there even a cooldown for teleporting to begin with? If you’re going to have a cooldown for teleporting, GIVE US A SPRINT BUTTON! BECAUSE YOU GENUINELY CANNOT ESCAPE THE ENEMIES FAST ENOUGH! 

OH AND CAN SOMEONE EXPLAIN HOW IT’S FAIR THAT YOU COULD BE TRYING TO TELEPORT AND THE ENEMY CAN SHOOT YOU, WHICH CAUSES YOUR TELEPORTATION TO START AT RED BEFORE GOING TO WHITE? 

Anyway this game is just awful and confusing, I would honestly rather play Dead Hook than this despite how glitchy that game was. At least I could actually play the game and not feel like I’m losing track of time because of stupid loading screens.

I genuinely don’t think the developers care at all, because I don’t see them responding to any of these reviews. The game’s been out for 2 years. It boggles my mind that someone looked at these things I just called out and said YUP THAT LOOKS GOOD. And then another person implemented it, and another person play tested it, and then you developers threw this game out there with all of these issues.

The game is great, mechanics are lovely, everything is perfect, but it would be so awesome if you could party up with other players and there could be this whole system where only one person can be one specific class, or it could just be an team of rifleman, or scouts, would really make the game more interesting, and there could be an feature where you could give your teammates equipment and weapons, ammo, ect, but you both have to be willing to give and accept trades so there can’t be any stealing, this includes anything you could find in the levels of the game, like if one person finds an item, it’s there item so the other players can’t use it unless that person lets them use it

reviews.review_title: Starts good, cracks start showing later
reviews.review_description:
Game feels great at first, but as you get deeper, it feels less and less fleshed out. Attachments are all boring flat damage buffs, and there aren't nearly enough of them. How are you going to have a sniper class with no scopes in the game? Tarot cards are not balanced in the slightest, with some like +magic damage on each shot vastly outshining stuff like healing on purify. There's only 3 bosses and you fight them in the same order every run. Magic wands feel like a waste of time and are unsatisfying to use. Breaking pots and opening boxes is mindless busywork, especially with the amount that are spawned per level. Dying respawns all enemies in the level, meaning you either have to run past them or waste several minutes clearing a level you've already done. This is especially egregious during boss fights. The health system becomes irrelevant on higher difficulties: there is a COD-style safety net which prevents you from dying instantly, instead turning your screen grey and healing you if you avoid damage for a few seconds. This makes being at full health pointless, especially since enemies do enough damage to take off 20+ hp per shot. 

The game also has some bugs and oversights that can range from mildly annoying to gamebreaking. Items falling through the floor, spawning in the ground/walls, etc. are minor and somewhat expected in VR. The more major problems have made me ragequit on several occasions. I've had invincible enemies spawn several times that I've had to ignore and hope they didn't chase me down. Healer enemies, for some reason, can heal each other and create an infinite healing loop which cannot be stopped no matter how many times you shoot them.

This game's concept has amazing potential, but the execution as of right now is just so lackluster. Both the roguelike elements and the guns (mainly attachments) need so much more work put into them for the gameplay loop to be fun.

reviews.review_title: Outstanding game; well worth your money!
reviews.review_description: This is an outstanding game with exceptional replayability. It's easily one of the best VR games I've ever played, and I'm greedily hoping the developers continue adding content to it. Well worth your money!

reviews.review_title: Great game, underrated for what it is
reviews.review_description: Gun mechanics and handling is clean and crisp, among the best physics I had ever experienced in VR games. The roguelike nature of this game is what pushes you to be cautious and mindful of your strategy, even though it  can really burden you. However, the saving grace after dying is that your ghost is able to detect all of the enemies which would make them easier to kill considering that you might have died a bit far into the level. Besides, you don't have to successfully complete an entire run to "progress" as you can use your rank points to upgrade your weapons, character, specific class abilities (which include upgrades for extra lives), or even upgrades that count towards EVERY class that aid you to victory. The variety of different classes caters to many different playstyles and each have unique abilities. This game is worth it for the sound effects, the animations, the clean graphics, music, and diverse biomes
Awesome game through and through, what more can I say.

The gun mechanics feel so good. This game is very addictive. I hate Rogue likes. But this game got me hooked. Shooting and reloading the rifle is so satisfying! I’ve been Holding off buying this game for a long time now. Didn’t think I would like it. So glad it got added to quest +.  Highly recommend this game if you love shooters in vr!

reviews.review_title: Light Brigade is peak standalone VR
reviews.review_description: This game is super polished, super fun and is just one of the best things available in standalone VR. If you’re in the fence about buying, just do it, you’ll love it!

May be clunky at first soon becomes Adept and skilled marksman. This game is a crawl into a sprint. The more you understand how that game functions and works the more prepared you feel and the farther you get, the bosses are a great change of pace and the grunts are nothing to laugh at. Every level you advance you pick up something new and exciting. I don’t see how you get bored with this game.

Satisfying gameplay, however setting sometimes tells a better story than dialogue or loading screens. It’d be nice to see more dark atmospheres like the fallen brigadiers you can encounter, covered in snow and sitting eerily comfortably. Something like broken machinery from wartime, sandbags considering the era of weaponry? I don’t know. I see so much potential in this game. Would love to see more. 
PS. Love the random lost soldier encounters. I always rush in Rambo style to go save them from the Obsidians.

The game isn't a bad game, but i have to face facts. I DONT like roguelites or roguelikes.
In death unchained was a way better game and I gave up on that one after lots of gameplay. 
This game isn't good enough to make me want to stick with it.
I don't understand the recent fascination with Roguelike games. Why do people like to grind their stats on a game, just to die hours into the game and start all over with slightly better stats?
I will stick to normal first person shooters and 3rd person games. There are too many good games out there to spend so much time redoing the same levels over and over in one game.

I’m am obsessed with this game. There are so many choices to choose from. I’m  a 60 year old woman and never thought a band bang game would be my go to game. I showed my son and he also thought it was fun. He teased me a little how I always got upset when he was younger playing video games and now I’m the one always playing video games😂😂

It feels great to play, and it's quite good for the most part. But it does suffer in two areas:

1. Variety - It feels like an early access game when it comes to the amount of content. It needs more enemies, weapons, classes, cards, zones and everything else

2. Meta progression - There just isn't enough to upgrade between runs, which coupled with the lack of variety and the fairly high difficulty, can turn into a slog

The foundation is great but it just needs more of everything. And no, releasing DLCs to fix this issue is not okay. If you released an incomplete game anything added needs to be at no additional cost until there is enough content in the base game.

They've been indeed releasing more content at no additional cost, but it's generally just one class and one zone, which doesn't really fix the core issues.

reviews.review_title: I wanted to love this game.
reviews.review_description:
When I started playing I thought I'd found a game I could grind and have fun in. But it crashes repeatedly after resets, reinstalls, factory resets... Through all these I've lost all progress multiple times.

Playing on quest 2 I find myself staring at "Cannot run, no memory" errors more times than not... The game is amazing, but I feel like the quest 3/S gets more love with these updates making the game near unplayable for anyone unable to upgrade....

The game itself is Great, extremely fun with seemingly a lot of replay ability.

The crashes just destroyed the immersion.

reviews.review_title: Should be much better than it is
reviews.review_description:
Played for an hour (on meta+) , the overall look of the game is very good, but the control really let it down. The teleport is almost impossible to direct when in combat, the rifle handles badly and when trying to pick things up half the time you end up grabbing items from your belt. 

if you can get around the controls, this would be a very good game, like I said it looks great and the atmosphere is perfect, just let down by poor mechanics

Don’t get me wrong, I absolutely love this game and I got really comfortable with the movement and gunplay. Unfortunately I just can’t recommend it in its current state as if you get too comfortable and move slightly faster than an npc, your weapon and hands will completely bug out, leaving you defenseless depending on what your loadout is. I’ve had multiple runs, at the time of writing this review, buried in the dirt because the game decided it was time to end it. Most recently, I was doing the Global Sun Run and made it to level 50/60 and my tarot cards wouldn’t activate and I wasn’t able to pick up any of the soul pockets the enemies drop.

All-in-all though, it’s a beautiful premise, I’ve made it to difficulty level 4 I think, and there’s a loadout that’ll fit what suits you best. The cons however are way to frequent for me to give it higher than one star, it just sucks having a run stolen from you when you know you can keep going.

reviews.review_title: Frustrating and not in the good way.
reviews.review_description:
For a game where you die so easily it's too buggy and unbalanced. Not only is your movement sluggish for how quickly you get swarmed the enemy's shots CLIP THROUGH COVER! Do you understand you frustrating it is to duck behind a corner or piller only to see the shots pass right through it. Roguelike games are supposed to make you feel stronger as you progress and stronger with each run but here only the enemies get tougher. The upgrades have little feel to them. Charms are ok but with long cool down times, not that there's anyway to tell. Sometimes the ground textures even go missing. I kept shooting a lower enemy not realizing there's a platform between us until I move there and boom it visible again. Reloading is a death sentence as there is no time to slugishly get to cover to fumble at your belt even if their shots don't just ignore it. That guy who sits by the entrance even clips through the floor half the time.
Edit: God the more I play the worse it gets. Now i got these teleporting bad guys that glitch out and become functionally immortal. You can't deal damage to them but they sure as he'll can still kill you. Also upgrade boxes have a habit of spawning in unreachable places. This level of unfixed bugs makes me think the higher scores are bots.

reviews.review_title: Long load times but fun gameplay
reviews.review_description:
I recently got a quest 3 and have been playing all different types of games. This is the only one so far that has caused me to need to hard reset. The load times approach close to a minute and then suddenly my headset freezes, sound cuts out, and it stops tracking. Going to give it a few more tries but I’ve already had to reboot twice due to this game.

Update: after trying again it seems like it does come out of the freeze eventually. It may just be longer load times then I’m used to and a quirk of the game.

Gameplay: solid shooting mechanics and good replay value due to the different classes. Haven’t gotten too far yet so I can’t say for sure everything the game has to offer, but so far it seems like a solid title.

This game has all the elements that make a game addictive. Responsive shooting mechanics, endless variety of levels, lots of different power ups and guns, rag doll physics, and my favourite thing of all, different classes to unlock. It all gives it so much playability. I was playing for about 3 hours off the bat and only scratched the surface. absolutely love it. One of the best VR games I've played.

Great gun game, well executed rogue mechanics. Love the small details like praying and the holographic animations on the player cards and unless I finally seemed to get over it, I'm getting long sessions in woth no motion sickness on smooth turning. It's just been a blast so far.

Listen I know that when you die you are supposed to spawn back at the start but, I just wish there could be a mode to go on forever and if you die just go back to your soul and revive and keep going. I just don’t like haveing to restart at the beginning and start all over again and I’m sure that other people can agree with me on this. Other than that, amazing, and a must buy!

This has been my go-to game for the last few weeks. I get the immersion I expected from VR. The levels have the right size, the enemies are well-balanced, the weapons feel authentic. My only complaint is occasional clipping (when moving with the controller!), and the 3D-boundaries of the scenery often does not match their shapes, allowing me to see the enemy but being protected by a non-existent wall/boulder/whatever. That also prevents me to aim/shot because the invisible object is on the way of my weapon.

reviews.review_title: theres only one reason this ain't a five
reviews.review_description: I love this game, best roguelike I have ever played, for *almost* all of it the difficulty is hard, but it isn't bs hard, its not going to do annoying stupid things to force it to be hard, its designed really well, its fun, amazing gunplay, rewarding progress, personally I have made it through about 66% of the game and have loved almost all of it, HOWEVER the one reason this is not a five star review is one of the bossfights, without spoilers, its BS hard, the arena is dark as all hell, and its clearly on purpose, because theres temporary trackers you can use around the arena to see where the boss is, but even the tracker is hard to see, the boss has a STUPID amount of health on top of a second phase, his sword attack just DECIDES when you go down to a single health, rather undodgeable, it leads to hiding behind the pillars, and dancing around them, only taking shots when you can, and running on 1-5 hp the whole fight, and getting the the second phase where he regenerates his entire health bar and starts summoning clones that ALL attack you and while each are one shot, they summon ALL the way around you, and he continues to spawn them in, I am stuck there, I have tried sniper, riflemen and bow all on it, and got nowhere, call it skill if you want, but the fight is just hard for all the wrong reasons, it wouldn't be HALF as hard if you could ACTUALLY see what your shooting at instead of blind firing and relying on luck to avoid his powerful sword attack, BESIDES that bossfight? best. vr game. EVER. imo

reviews.review_title: One of the best games I have ever played
reviews.review_description: This game is fun and has a lot of replayability and the enemies are random so you can't have the same run over and over and the maps are randomly generated as well and I hope this game keeps on getting updated and I hope it keeps on growing in popularity.

reviews.review_title: If I could I’d join the brigade
reviews.review_description: If you like roguelikes ww2 or magic (or your Cristian) you will LOVE this game it’s hard at first don’t get me wrong but after you get used to it and upgrade your weapons a bit it’s easy as hell warning it’s almost all German guns .but I have 2 questions why isn’t their a machine gunner class and 2 why aren’t you already playing it

I absolutely love dungeon delving games that allow you to upgrade the further you go on by choosing perk cards. I can't get enough of games like that, and bonus because this is in VR and I mixed military so I have great shooting capabilities and cover to cover movements. 
The one thing I don't like is that you can't just return back to the temple after every run, you have to die for some reason. On my first run I made it all the way to the 5th floor and thought it would be wise to end the run and return back to the temple just to find out that when you do that you're pretty much just offing yourself. I don't think that should be a thing.  I believe that you should be able to return to the temple on your own accord to heal and prep for your next run without your character having to die. 
Other than that this is definitely going to be one of the top tier VR games in my library that I will always come back to play because it's just that fun. I'm looking forward to any co-op or multiplayer functionalities into the future.
Also, can we please get a melee feature added into the game? 
It's needed.

I went into The Light Brigade not knowing exactly what to expect, and at first, I found myself a little frustrated while getting used to the mechanics. But once I got the hang of things, the game really started to shine.

The combination of tactical shooting, teleportation-based movement, and roguelike progression makes for an engaging and rewarding experience. Landing headshots feels incredibly satisfying, and the strategic elements of positioning and weapon handling add depth.

Visually, the game does a great job with its atmospheric environments, and the immersive gunplay makes every run feel intense. However, the early learning curve might be a bit steep for some players.

This game is so fun! The gun play and perks are great! The vibe of the atmosphere is awesome  too. All the extra classes help add more content. I hope they make a sequel or maybe add paid coop! I will gladly support these devs on any content they want to add to this game / universe!

I definitely recommend it to anyone.

Way too hard! The game doesn't give me a chance to get used to it before throwing enemies my way that slaughtered me in seconds. This was on the lowest difficulty. No balance whatsoever - pathetic. Not interested.

Shaped up to be the AAA game that all the big AAA studios keep getting wrong. Been playing this since my Q2 days and its only gotten more and more polished and replayable since it first came out. These developers CARE about their game passionately and have been updating/improving/ adding content non stop as they actually listen to their community. Puts other big studios to shame. Aside from being the most addictive rogue-lite on the store, it also has the most solid and satisfying shooting and gun handling of anything on the store. The looting and tarot upgrade boxes keep you snooping every corner of the maps. Enemy AI rises to the challenge of your level. Cant say enough good. This is a game I play regularly for years (out of 100+ titles I own) and Im still not done unlocking everything and all of the new content. Its still fun every time. Bravo

Simple, satisfying gunplay, tense—but not oppressive—atmosphere, a difficulty forgiving enough that even the most crushing defeat doesn't feel unfair, and enough cool trinkets and upgrades that a lucky run can see you one-shotting grunts with a 1911 before the second boss... I'm enjoying this one. It would be a blast with multiplayer, but as it is I've got a lot more to enjoy yet!

reviews.review_title: Too good to be a free title
reviews.review_description: I got this as a free title for the meta quest 3 + subscription and I didn’t expect much. But this has been one of my favorite games I have so far and I look forward to this game s lot!
I like this game and strawberries about the same.  I’m not sure how else to put it.

Absolutely loving it, so far the BEST game on Quest for me. 
I see myself playing it far more often than I do other games and pretty much the only game that keeps me coming back. 

Highly recommended!

Feels like Until You Fall with guns. Cool atmosphere with great music, sound and gun handling. Very well done. Can’t wait to see what Quest 3 will do for this game. Recommended if you like Rogue-likes and shooters.

(I’m adding this part in response to other reviews citing lack of info on the upgrade cards you find around the levels. While holding the card, turn your wrist like you’re going to look at your watch - a more lengthy description of what the card does will appear. You can also do this with any gems or gun attachments you find as well)

I will never understand how you can make a game where you can see other peoples places of death (their name included) but not have multiplayer for a game like this. Fun game, but without the multiplayer it just makes no sense to me why I can see other players names n places of death, but not being able to play a game with others. Underwhelming.
I love rogue like type game, this is so far the best I have play on the vr. The game have great art style too
I've played tons of this game, it's really fun
I truly enjoy playing this game. Not sure if there’s a feature to check your stats per level. If not, adding one would be a great addition.
